chat.title: AI Chat
chat.model_hint: Selected model will be saved as default
chat.select_model_warning: Please select a model in Chat Settings
chat.models_list_hint: List shows models from local caches: Foundry from /foundry/models/cached, HuggingFace from /hf/models, llama.cpp from /llama/models (.gguf in ~/.models).
chat.models_switch_hint: On model switch, active models are unloaded (Foundry unload + HF unload) and llama.cpp is stopped if needed, then the selected model is loaded.
chat.models_add_hint: To add: use Foundry, HuggingFace or llama.cpp tabs. For llama.cpp copy .gguf to ~/.models then Refresh.
chat.hf_step2: For gated models set token in Settings → HuggingFace and accept license.
chat.hf_step4: Click Refresh in Downloaded Locally — model appears in chat list.

models.title: Connected Models
models.no_models: No models connected. Use the Foundry tab to load models.

foundry.models_placeholder: Click "List Models" to see available models
foundry.downloaded_hint: Only already downloaded models from the local Foundry cache can be loaded.
foundry.downloaded_placeholder: Click "Refresh Downloaded" to see cached models
foundry.cli_howto: How to download a model via Foundry CLI

rag.no_bases: No RAG bases yet. Build one →
rag.already_indexed: Index already exists for this directory. Use Rebuild (force) to re-index.
rag.ready_to_build: No index yet — ready to build.

settings.title: Configuration
settings.dir_hint: ~/ is the user home directory. Directories are created automatically on first use.
settings.clear_rag_hint: Remove all indexed documents from the RAG system
settings.llama_autostart_hint: Saves to config.json: llama_cpp.auto_start and llama_cpp.model_path
settings.tip_host: Bind address. Use 0.0.0.0 to accept connections from all interfaces
settings.tip_mode: dev enables auto-reload; prod disables it
settings.tip_auto_port: Automatically find a free port if the configured one is busy
settings.tip_dir_models: Directory for GGUF model files used by llama.cpp
settings.tip_dir_rag: Directory where FAISS RAG indexes are stored
settings.tip_foundry_url: Base URL of the Foundry Local service. Leave empty for auto-discovery
settings.tip_foundry_model: Model ID to load by default. Use prefix hf:: or llama:: for other backends
settings.tip_max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens in the generated response
settings.tip_foundry_autoload: Automatically load the default model when the server starts
settings.tip_rag_enabled: Enable the FAISS vector search system for context injection
settings.tip_rag_index: Directory where the FAISS index files are stored
settings.tip_rag_chunk: Size of text chunks when indexing documents
settings.tip_llama_port: Port for the llama-server.exe HTTP API (default: 9780)
settings.tip_llama_model_path: Full path to the .gguf model file
settings.tip_llama_autostart: Start llama-server.exe automatically when FastAPI Foundry starts
settings.tip_hf_device: Device for HuggingFace inference: auto detects GPU if available
settings.tip_docs_port: Port for the MkDocs documentation server
settings.tip_docs_enabled: Start MkDocs server automatically on startup
settings.tip_api_key: Require this key in X-API-Key header. Leave empty to disable auth
settings.tip_debug: Enable FastAPI debug mode with detailed error pages
settings.tip_verbose: Log additional details for each request
settings.hf_token_moved: HuggingFace Token has been moved to the
settings.translator_enabled_hint: Translates user input → English before sending to model, then translates response back to user language.
settings.tip_translator_provider: mymemory and libretranslate are free and require no API key
settings.tip_translator_timeout: Maximum wait time for translation request in seconds
settings.tip_translator_libre_url: URL of your LibreTranslate instance or the public one

llama.gguf_what: GGUF is a binary model format for local inference, used by llama.cpp and compatible tools.
llama.gguf_hf_hint: Search by keywords: gguf, llama gguf, mistral gguf. Files look like: model.Q4_K_M.gguf
llama.gguf_lmstudio: 2. LM Studio — built-in catalog, download via GUI.
llama.gguf_ollama: 3. Ollama — downloads models automatically (ollama pull), but does not expose the file directly.
llama.gguf_browser_label: Via browser: open repository → select .gguf → download.
llama.gguf_run_hint: Or via GUI: LM Studio, Text Generation WebUI.

agent.title: AI Agent
agent.placeholder: Describe a task for the agent...
agent.start_prompt: Send a task to the agent
agent.ks_session_hint: List of key-press durations in ms
